docs.title: docmetrics
docs.text:
DocMetrics is a tool to analyze and measure the complexity and usefulness of documentation.

It does this using a set of multiple choice questions, that you wish your users to be able to answer after reading the documentation.

An LLM is then asked to answer these questions, with and without the documentation as part of its context. The difference in score is then used as a measure of the usefulness of the documentation.

The list of questions need to be provided to the tool by passing a --questions option pointing to a yaml file. See the sample_questions.yaml file for an example.

The choice of LLM can be configured, but defaults to Google's Gemini model. The set of tools available to the agent can also be configured.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
1. (optional) Install UV: https://docs.astral.sh/uv/getting-started/installation/

  2. Install this package:

uv tool install docmetrics

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
To see available options, run:

uv run docmetrics --help

To test it out, you first need to setup the GEMINI_API_KEY environment variable with your Google API key. Then, you can run:

uv run docmetrics --questions sample_questions.yaml

docs.title: score property
docs.text:
score: float

The percentage of questions that the LLM answered correctly.

docs.title: question instance-attribute
docs.text:
question: str

The question to ask the LLM.

docs.title: options instance-attribute
docs.text:
options: dict[Letter, str]

A list of possible answers to the question.

docs.title: answer instance-attribute
docs.text:
answer: Letter

The correct answer to the question (must be one of the letters in options).

docs.title: docs_urls class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
docs_urls: list[str] | None = None

A list of URLs to relevant documentation pages.

The hope is that by reading these pages, the LLM can answer the question more accurately. Note that the LLMs are not going to look at links within these pages, only the content of these pages itself. For example, giving a link to docs.mila.quebec wouldn't be very helpful.

docs.title: justification class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
justification: str = ''

A brief justification for the selected answer.

docs.title: evaluate_llm
docs.text:
evaluate_llm(
    questions: list[Question], with_docs: bool, model: str
) -> EvaluationResult

Evaluates an LLM on some questions with/without documentation as context.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default questions list[Question] required with_docs bool required model str required

Returns:

Type Description The evaluation results. Notes - Could also input a list of tools to give to the agent, in addition to URL search.

docs.title: ask_question
docs.text:
ask_question(
    client: Client | None,
    question: Question,
    with_docs: bool,
    model: str,
    tools: list[Tool | Callable] | None,
) -> bool | None

Asks a question to the LLM and returns whether the LLM answered correctly.

Returns None if the LLM's answer was invalid.
